strength.title: Peak Strength
strength.stimulus: Heaviest loads of the block. Fewer reps, higher intensity. Build peak strength before deload.

structure.heading: Warm-up (12 min)
items: Progressive loading: 4 sets building to working weight

structure.heading: Back Squat (22 min)
items: Heaviest squats of the block — 1 rep in reserve

notes.about: Heaviest week of the block. You should feel strong today — the previous two weeks built to this. RPE 8 means you have ONE rep in reserve. Don't grind to failure. Form breakdown on heavy sets leads to injury. If form goes, rack the bar, rest longer, and try again.
notes.week_context: Peak week — lowest reps, highest loads. This is the payoff of the previous two weeks.
notes.block_context: Third of four weeks. Next week is deload — recovery before the next block starts.

running.title: Race-Pace Intervals
running.stimulus: Practice your target race pace in short, repeatable intervals. Today teaches your legs what race effort feels like.

structure.heading: Warm-up (15 min)
items: 5 min dynamic drills: A-skips, B-skips, strides x 4

structure.heading: Main Session (35 min)
items: Rest 2 min jog recovery between intervals

notes.about: Peak running week. These intervals should match your goal race pace. The first two feel easy — resist speeding up. The last two are where real adaptation happens — when your legs hurt and you maintain pace, that's fitness being built. Consistency across all 6 matters more than fastest splits.
notes.week_context: Peak week — highest intensity of the block. Recover well between intervals.
notes.block_context: Running peak. Next week drops volume and intensity.

engine.title: Race-Pace Station Circuit
engine.stimulus: Full station circuit at race pace. This is your dress rehearsal.

structure.heading: Warm-up (10 min)
items: Practice: 5 wall balls, 5 burpees, 10m sled push light

structure.heading: Main Session (40 min)
items: Treat this as race simulation — pace like you'd pace on race day

notes.about: Peak engine session. This is the closest you'll get to race conditions in the block. Pay attention: how does your breathing recover between stations? How does your pace drift across the 3 rounds? These answers tell you how to race. If round 3 falls apart, your round 1 pace was too hot — learn that lesson now, not on race day.
notes.week_context: Peak week — race-pace effort. Full focus on race-specific output.
notes.block_context: Third week of the block. Deload next week allows recovery.

emom.title: Peak EMOM
emom.stimulus: Highest work density EMOM of the block. Race-pace reps.

structure.heading: EMOM 32 min
items: Race pace — 5-10 sec rest per work minute

notes.about: Peak EMOM. You should barely finish each minute in time. If you're finishing with 20+ seconds to spare, you're pacing too easy for peak week. If you can't finish a minute, scale — but aim for the edge of what you can sustain. This builds race-day pacing intuition.
notes.week_context: Peak — highest density. Earn your deload next week.

simulation.title: Race-Pace Simulation
simulation.stimulus: Full race simulation at race pace. Your dress rehearsal for race day.

structure.heading: Warm-up (10 min)
items: 5 min easy jog building to tempo
Practice: 5 wall balls, light sled push 20m

structure.heading: Main Session (45 min)
items: Full Hyrox race simulation at race pace (scaled to 40 min):
No rest between stations — full race transitions
If you can't finish in 40 min, scale distance or intensity

notes.about: Dress rehearsal. Treat this like race day — warm up properly, fuel and hydrate beforehand, and execute. The lessons from this session will sharpen your race strategy more than anything else in the block. Record your splits and station times. Next block, we'll beat these numbers.
notes.week_context: Peak week simulation — race-pace effort across full race format.
notes.block_context: Third week. After this, deload and recover.

notes.about: Peak week. Highest intensity of the block across all sessions.
notes.week_context: Peak: heaviest loads, fastest paces, race-pace intervals.
notes.block_context: Week 3 of 4. Deload next week gives your body time to absorb these gains.
